title: Gerbner–Keszegh–Methuku–Abhishek–Nagy–Patkós–Tompkins–Xiao Conjecture
statement_text: The size of pairs of set systems satisfying both the Two Families hypotheses of Bollob\'{a}s and an intersection condition on the first family is bounded by the Ahlswede-Khachatrian bound on the size of $t$-intersecting families.
context_text: Gerbner, Keszegh, Methuku, Abhishek, Nagy, Patk\'{o}s, Tompkins, and Xiao conjectured a bound for pairs of families satisfying simultaneously the Bollob\'{a}s Two Families condition and a cross-intersection condition. Section 5 of the paper is devoted to its affirmative resolution.
attributed_to: Gerbner, Keszegh, Methuku, Abhishek, Nagy, Patkós, Tompkins, and Xiao
paper_contribution: The authors affirmatively resolve the conjecture, proving that such pairs of families are bounded in size by the Ahlswede-Khachatrian bound for $t$-intersecting families.
notes: Exact LaTeX statement not reproduced verbatim in the provided excerpt; paraphrased from the abstract and the Section 5 overview in the introduction. PDF source.
paper_title: Combinatorics in the exterior algebra and the Bollobás Two Families Theorem

title: Question on extremal self-annihilating subspaces (Theorem 2.3)
statement_text: For $r < n/2$, are all extremal self-annihilating subspaces $W \subseteq \bigwedge^r V$ with $\dim W = \binom{n-1}{r-1}$ of the form $\{v \wedge z : z \in \bigwedge^{r-1} V\}$ for some fixed $v \in V$?
context_text: Theorem 2.3 shows that a self-annihilating subspace $W \subseteq \bigwedge^r V$ satisfies $\dim W \leq \binom{n-1}{r-1}$ for $r \leq n/2$, with equality achieved by spaces of the form $\{v \wedge z : z \in \bigwedge^{r-1} V\}$. The authors verify this characterization for $r = 1$ and $r = 2$ and ask whether it holds in general.
notes: Stated in running prose after Theorem 2.3: 'It is an interesting question to describe the extremal examples for Theorem 2.3 ... Could all extremal examples be of this form?' PDF source.
paper_title: Combinatorics in the exterior algebra and the Bollobás Two Families Theorem

title: Question on extremal mutually annihilating pairs (Theorem 2.4)
statement_text: For $r, s < n/2$, characterize all pairs of subspaces $U \subseteq \bigwedge^r V$ and $W \subseteq \bigwedge^s V$ that are mutually annihilating and attain $\dim U \cdot \dim W = \binom{n-1}{r-1}\binom{n-1}{s-1}$.
context_text: Theorem 2.4 bounds $\dim U \cdot \dim W$ for mutually annihilating pairs $U \subseteq \bigwedge^r V$, $W \subseteq \bigwedge^s V$ with $r, s \leq n/2$. Equality is achieved by fixing $v \in V$ and setting $U = \{v \wedge z : z \in \bigwedge^{r-1} V\}$ and $W = \{v \wedge z : z \in \bigwedge^{s-1} V\}$, and the authors ask for a full characterization of all extremal examples.
notes: Stated in running prose after Theorem 2.4: 'it would be interesting to characterize the extremal examples when $r, s < n/2$.' PDF source.
paper_title: Combinatorics in the exterior algebra and the Bollobás Two Families Theorem
